title: Gardens in the Sky: Salesforce Transit Center and Rooftop Park Tour
content: # Gardens in the Sky: Salesforce Transit Center and Rooftop Park. Stretching for four blocks, the Salesforce Transit Center is a unique structure anchoring a new neighborhood and connecting San Francisco to its suburbs and beyond. Most enchanting (and unknown to many locals) is its rooftop park, which showcases multiple botanical gardens, a redwood forest, a gondola, and a captivating fountain. Stroll with us through this San Francisco landmark, where we will see the transit center’s soaring Grand Hall, experience its multimedia public art, walk among hundreds of trees and plants, and take in alluring skyline views. The tour is flat and includes use of elevators, escalators, and/or a gondola lift. This tour is given in collaboration with Transbay Joint Powers Authority (TJPA), which owns and operates the Salesforce Transit Center. Meet in Salesforce Plaza on the South West corner of Mission and Fremont street, by the sky gondola. Please note: Private group tours required for groups of 7 or more.

title: Salesforce rooftop park? - San Francisco Forum - Tripadvisor
content: It's free. Never heard of a tour of the park, although that doesn't mean there isn't one. It's not that huge.

title: Salesforce Park | Transbay Joint Powers Authority
content: ###### **Salesforce Park Hours:** May 1 - October 31 | 6 a.m. to 9 p.m. / November 1 - April 30 | 6 a.m. to 8 p.m. Everyone’s welcome to enjoy this 5.4 acre public park, a living roof where a curved walking trail lined with benches surrounds grassy lawns, dancing fountains, a children’s play area, and an amphitheater. While we love our animal friends, they are not allowed at Salesforce Park, which is home to delicate plants and habitat and 13 unique ecosystems. Seventy feet above the Grand Hall, the Park runs the entire length of the Transit Center’s nearly four-block stretch. Home to 600 trees and 16,000 plants arranged in 13 different botanical feature areas, this iconic public park destination in the San Francisco Bay Area is open and enjoyment of all...and there’s nothing else like it anywhere. ### Discover what’s at Salesforce Park. On a rooftop park that’s designed for the enjoyment and benefit of all, children and a climbing rope frame are essential ingredients.
